results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DESIGN AND DEVELOP A NOVEL METHOD TO EFFICIENTLY EXTRACT LITHIUM FROM A GEOTHERMAL BRINE WITH MINIMAL WATER LOSS.ACTIVITIES:SPECIFICALLY, RESEARCHERS PROPOSE TO DEVELOP A NOVEL ZIRCONIUM-DOPED COMPOUND THAT AS THE POTENTIAL TO INCREASE THE ADSORPTION CAPACITY OF LITHIUM FROM BRINE. THE RESEARCH OBJECTIVES ARE TO: 1) SYNTHESIZE AND CHARACTERIZE THE LITHIUM-ION SIEVE (LI4MN4.9ZR0.1O12); 2) CONVERT THE LITHIUM ION TO THE MANGANESE ION WHICH IS THE ADSORBENT MATERIAL FOR THE LITHIUM EXTRACTION AND CONDUCT ADSORPTION-DESORPTION PROCESS; AND 3) PURIFY THE SALT PRECIPITATES TO PRODUCE LITHIUM CARBONATE.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES WILL INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS, AS WELL AS JOURNAL ARTICLES ON EFFORTS TO SYNTHESIZE THE NOVEL ZIRCONIUM COMPOUND AND INVESTIGATE THE EFFECTIVENESS OF USING AS AN ADSORBENT FOR THE PROCESS OF LITHIUM EXTRACTION. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE THE LITHIUM MINING AND BATTERY INDUSTRIES, ELECTRIC VEHICLE MANUFACTURERS, WATER QUALITY MANAGERS, AND COMMUNITIES CURRENTLY AFFECTED BY LITHIUM MINING POLLUTION.
